specific_location: South end of alfalfa field at Chico Basin Ranch, the Pueblo portion.
description: A wood-pewee by long wings (longer than any Empid) and bill shape and posing on an exposed limb in the open. Identified by song, but looking at my photographs the bird shows the lower bill mandible is mostly all orange with just a tiny dark tip. The bird was less vested (see photos) than the common Western Wood-Pewee that was absent from this site. The two whitish wingbars were prominent and contrasted with a mostly light gray body that had some hints of green.Behaviors: Perched high in a cottonwood snag, up about 80 feet and then flew lower in a giant plains cottonwood that is living. Sang periodically which is how I saw the bird after being alerted by song. Stayed for up to a minute at a time perched in the same spot, then fly-caught and returned to same perch or one nearby. Disappeared for a few minutes when it moved to a living tree.Call: singing - slow peee-a-weee with the weee drawn out. After a long pause it also then gave a down-slurred per-yer, also draw out. Repeated but long time before songs given, as much as 2-3 minutes.Plumage: adult
